review_questions: Does CP00-150 cover exactly the 10 planned Risk A units in order?
Can any CP00-150 row materialize Claude prompts, record human escalation, or load fixture payloads?
Can any CP00-150 row evaluate permission/audit binding, failure taxonomy, or execute failure recovery?
Can any CP00-150 row write audit/product state, emit receipts/evidence, call APIs, render UI, or expose hidden internals?
Does CP00-150 hand off cleanly from CP00-149 to CP00-151 without implementing LDIP or splitting HRX?

finding.title: Several readiness/attestation booleans are hardcoded literals and asserted as literals
finding.detail: In createAuditComplianceCp150FailureFixtureSensitiveBoundary() the flags permission_audit_sensitive_terminal_declared, synthetic_fixture_failure_opening_declared, sensitive_failure_taxonomy_boundary_declared, missing_context_failure_boundaries_declared, cross_tenant_permission_denied_boundaries_declared, fixture_payload_non_materialization_declared, claude_edge_case_prompt_boundary_declared, human_escalation_note_boundary_declared, hidden_field_policy_declared, h03_gate_bound, c03_gate_bound, cp149_handoff_inherited, cp151_handoff_declared are returned as constant true, and the freezeNoWriteResult no_write_attestation flags are unconditionally false. The unit tests and validator assert those same literals, so they verify object shape rather than detecting a behavioral regression. The genuinely load-bearing checks (covered_unit count/first/last/uniqueness, plan-order comparison, and the countBy-derived deliverable_counts {claude_review:1,implementation:1,failure_recovery:7,security_audit:1} and evidence_mode_counts {terminal:2,fixture_opening:8}) ARE data-derived and would catch a real drift, so this is non-blocking — but the assertion suite is weaker than its surface area suggests.
finding.recommendation: Derive at least the distribution/gate/handoff flags from row data so the tests fail on regression rather than restating constants.

finding.title: Hermes evidence packet references a cp00-150 manifest not produced by this pack
finding.detail: createAuditComplianceCp150HermesEvidencePacket lists 'docs/closeout-packs/cp00-150/manifest.json' in evidence_refs, but neither the implementation summary nor the diff creates that file. The architecture validator and the new unit test both read it inside try/catch and fall back to the closeout-pack-plan.json CP00-150 entry, so the dangling reference is tolerated and validation still passes — but the evidence_ref is currently aspirational rather than backed by a real artifact.

finding.title: case_id and catalog_id are identical for every row, making the dual-key lookup redundant
finding.detail: buildRows() sets case_id and catalog_id to the same `${sourceUnitId}.${behaviorKind}` string, so the `candidate.case_id === caseId || candidate.catalog_id === caseId` branch in runAuditComplianceCp150FailureFixtureSensitiveBoundaryCase never exercises its second clause. Cosmetic only.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-150 covers exactly the 10 planned Risk A units RP03.P07.M05.S21–RP03.P07.M06.S08 in plan order: createAuditComplianceCp150CoveredUnitIds() derives S21,S22,M06.S01..S08 from CP150_MICRO_SLICES (unit_start 21 then 1) and validateAuditComplianceCp150Coverage enforces count=10, first=S21, last=S08, uniqueness, and element-wise plan-order match, mirrored in both the unit test and the architecture validator. Q1 satisfied. Q2: the new module is inert by construction — no fs/network/DOM/eval/import side effects; every export returns a frozen descriptor and freezeNoWriteResult stamps the full no-write/non-execution attestation, so the no-write, no-fixture-payload-load/materialize, no-permission/audit/taxonomy-evaluation, no-failure-recovery, no-Claude-prompt-materialize/send, no-human-escalation, no-API/UI/network, no-LDIP, and no-HRX-split claims hold genuinely, not just by assertion. Q3: contract bumped to v0.17 with current_pack→CP00-150 (Risk A, 10 units) and next CP00-151/RP03.P07.M06.S09; H03 Hermes packet, C03 Claude packet (claude-opus-4-8, max, read_only, executes_review=false, sends_claude_prompt=false), and the CP00-149→CP00-150→CP00-151 handoff chain are internally consistent (CP00-149.next_subphase S21 = CP00-150 first unit; CP00-150 last unit S08 → next S09). Q4: validator asserts schema_version, current_pack fields, sensitive-boundary pack contract_id/flag/source/count, public exports, hidden-source-field subset, full no_write_attestation, plan coverage, and per-case non-execution; the deliverable/evidence-mode distributions are data-derived. Findings are all non-blocking: P2 notes that many readiness/attestation booleans are hardcoded-and-asserted (shape, not behavior), and P3 notes a dangling cp00-150 manifest evidence_ref (tolerated via plan fallback) and a redundant case_id/catalog_id lookup. No P0/P1. Recommend production ready.
